articles.title: 36 Malicious npm Packages Exploited Redis, PostgreSQL to Deploy Persistent Implants
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 36 malicious packages in the npm registry that are disguised as Strapi CMS plugins but come with different payloads to facilitate Redis and PostgreSQL exploitation, deploy reverse shells, harvest credentials, and drop a persistent implant.  "Every package contains three files (package.json, index.js, postinstall.js), has no description, repository, or homepage, and uses version 3.6.8 to appear as a mature Strapi v3 community plugin," SafeDep said .  All identified npm packages follow the same naming convention, starting with "strapi-plugin-" and then phrases like "cron," "database," or "server" to fool unsuspecting developers into downloading them. It's worth noting that the official Strapi plugins are scoped under "@strapi/."  The packages, uploaded by four sock puppet accounts "umarbek1233," "kekylf12," "tikeqemif26," and "umar_bektembiev1...
articles.title_fr: 36 paquets npm malveillants ont exploité Redis et PostgreSQL pour déployer des implants persistants
articles.summary_fr: Des chercheurs en cybersécurité ont découvert 36 paquets malveillants dans le registre npm, déguisés en plugins Strapi CMS. Ces paquets contiennent diverses charges utiles pour exploiter Redis et PostgreSQL, déployer des shells inversés, collecter des identifiants et installer des implants persistants. Ils utilisent une convention de nommage spécifique ("strapi-plugin-...") et la version 3.6.8 pour tromper les développeurs.

articles.title: Fortinet Patches Actively Exploited CVE-2026-35616 in FortiClient EMS
articles.summary: Fortinet has released out-of-band patches for a critical security flaw impacting FortiClient EMS that it said has been exploited in the wild.  The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-35616  (CVSS score: 9.1), has been described as a pre-authentication API access bypass leading to privilege escalation.  "An improper access control vulnerability [CWE-284] in FortiClient EMS may allow an unauthenticated attacker to execute unauthorized code or commands via crafted requests," Fortinet said  in a Saturday advisory.  The issue affects FortiClient EMS versions 7.4.5 through 7.4.6. It's expected to be fully patched in the upcoming version 7.4.7, although the company has released a hotfix  to address it.    Simo Kohonen from Defused Cyber and Nguyen Duc Anh have been credited with discovering and reporting the flaw. In a post on X, Defused Cyber said  it observed zero-day exploitation of CVE-2026-35616 earlier this week. Accor...
articles.title_fr: Fortinet corrige la vulnérabilité CVE-2026-35616 activement exploitée dans FortiClient EMS
articles.summary_fr: Fortinet a publié des correctifs urgents pour une faille de sécurité critique (CVE-2026-35616, score CVSS 9.1) affectant FortiClient EMS, qui a été exploitée dans la nature. Cette vulnérabilité de contrôle d'accès inapproprié permet à un attaquant non authentifié d'exécuter du code ou des commandes non autorisées. Elle concerne les versions 7.4.5 à 7.4.6 et un correctif a été mis à disposition en attendant la version 7.4.7.

articles.title: China-Linked TA416 Targets European Governments with PlugX and OAuth-Based Phishing
articles.summary: A China-aligned threat actor has set its sights on European government and diplomatic organizations since mid-2025, following a two-year period of minimal targeting in the region.  The campaign has been attributed to TA416 , a cluster of activity that overlaps with DarkPeony, RedDelta, Red Lich, SmugX, UNC6384, and Vertigo Panda.  "This TA416 activity included multiple waves of web bug and malware delivery campaigns against diplomatic missions to the European Union and NATO across a range of European countries," Proofpoint researchers Mark Kelly and Georgi Mladenov said .  "Throughout this period, TA416 regularly altered its infection chain, including abusing Cloudflare Turnstile challenge pages, abusing OAuth redirects, and using C# project files, as well as frequently updating its custom PlugX payload."  TA416 has also been observed orchestrating multiple campaigns aimed at diplomatic and government entities in the Middle East following the outbreak...
articles.title_fr: Le groupe TA416, lié à la Chine, cible les gouvernements européens avec PlugX et des attaques de phishing basées sur OAuth
articles.summary_fr: Un acteur de menace lié à la Chine, nommé TA416, cible les organisations gouvernementales et diplomatiques européennes depuis mi-2025, après une période d'activité réduite. Ce groupe mène des campagnes de distribution de logiciels malveillants et de web bugs contre les missions diplomatiques auprès de l'UE et de l'OTAN. TA416 modifie régulièrement sa chaîne d'infection, utilisant notamment des redirections OAuth et mettant à jour sa charge utile PlugX personnalisée.

script:
Bonjour et bienvenue dans votre Flash Info Cyber du jour, le rendez-vous incontournable pour rester informé et sécurisé dans le monde numérique !

Commençons avec une alerte importante pour les développeurs. Des chercheurs
